A test script that asserts a function returns 1 when called with specific parameters.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the return value of an immediately invoked function expression (IIFE). The function takes two parameters, `param1` and `param2`, and always returns the integer `1`. The IIFE is called with the arguments `true` and `"blah"`, and the result is compared to `1` using the `assert` function. The purpose of this code is to verify that the function consistently returns `1`, regardless of the input parameters. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a single assertion test.
